title: From requirements to API endpoints
intro: The disciplined path that turns fuzzy requirements into a concrete API contract.
prompt: Drag into order — the earliest step first.

steps.text: Identify actors — every external person or system that interacts with it
Enumerate use cases — the meaningful interactions, success and failure paths
Model each use case as a UML sequence diagram over time
Find the messages that cross the system boundary
Turn each boundary-crossing message into a public API endpoint

debrief: Actors bound the problem; use cases capture the interactions; sequence diagrams make the message flow explicit over time. The key move is the last two: every message that crosses the *system boundary* is exactly a public API endpoint. This is why API design isn't guesswork — it's a mechanical translation of the use cases you already modelled.
